title: Boxplot Database Backend Support Analysis
text: Current: Recommended:
code: It has been tested with the following connections: - MS SQL Server - PostgreSQL - Oracle - sparklyr Boxplot functions require database support for percentile/quantile calculations.  **Supported databases** (tested): - Spark/Hive (via sparklyr) - uses `percentile_approx()` - SQL Server (2012+) - uses `PERCENTILE_CONT()` - PostgreSQL (9.4+) - uses `percentile_cont()` - Oracle (9i+) - uses `PERCENTILE_CONT()`  **Not supported**: - SQLite (no percentile functions) - MySQL < 8.0 (no percentile functions) - MariaDB (limited/no percentile support)  **Note**: For unsupported backends, consider using `db_compute_bins()` for histograms as an alternative visualization.

text: Add NEWS.md:
code: # dbplot 0.4.0  ## Breaking Changes  * Package no longer re-exports the `%>%` pipe operator. Users should use R's   native `|>` pipe (available in R >= 4.1.0) or load magrittr explicitly if   they prefer `%>%`.  ## Improvements  * Updated minimum R version to 4.1.0 * Updated all package dependencies to modern versions (dplyr >= 1.0.0, rlang >= 1.0.0) * Added cli package for better error messages * All examples now use native pipe `|>` instead of `%>%` * Fixed typos in code and documentation

text: default dbplot_histogram() creates 30 bin histogram  Use binwidth fix bin size  outputs ggplot2 object, customization can done
code: library(ggplot2)  db_flights |>    dbplot_histogram(distance) db_flights |>    dbplot_histogram(distance, binwidth = 400) db_flights |>    dbplot_histogram(distance, binwidth = 400) +   labs(title = "Flights - Distance traveled") +   theme_bw()

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: visualize two continuous variables, typically resort Scatter plot. However, may practical visualizing millions billions dots representing intersections two variables. Raster plot may better option, concentrates intersections squares easier parse visually. Raster plot basically Histogram. takes two continuous variables creates discrete 2-dimensional bins represented squares plot. determines either number rows inside square processes aggregation, like average. fill argument passed, default calculation count, n()  Pass aggregation formula can run inside database  Increase decrease , less, definition. resolution argument controls , defaults 100
code: db_flights |>   dbplot_raster(sched_dep_time, sched_arr_time) db_flights |>   dbplot_raster(     sched_dep_time,      sched_arr_time,      mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE)     ) db_flights |>   dbplot_raster(     sched_dep_time,      sched_arr_time,      mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE),     resolution = 20     )

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: dbplot_bar() defaults count() value discrete variable  Pass aggregation formula calculated value discrete variable
code: db_flights |>   dbplot_bar(origin) db_flights |>   dbplot_bar(origin, avg_delay =  mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: dbplot_line() defaults count() value discrete variable  Pass formula operated value discrete variable
code: db_flights |>   dbplot_line(month) db_flights |>   dbplot_line(month, avg_delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: expects discrete variable group , continuous variable calculate percentiles IQR. doesn’t calculate outliers. Boxplot functions require database support percentile/quantile calculations. Supported databases: Spark/Hive (via sparklyr) - uses percentile_approx() SQL Server (2012+) - uses PERCENTILE_CONT() PostgreSQL (9.4+) - uses percentile_cont() Oracle (9i+) - uses PERCENTILE_CONT() supported: SQLite, MySQL < 8.0, MariaDB (percentile functions) example using dbplot_boxplot() local data frame:
code: nycflights13::flights |>   dbplot_boxplot(origin, distance)

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: customized plot needed, data underpins plots can also accessed: db_compute_bins() - Returns data frame bins count per bin db_compute_count() - Returns data frame count per discrete value db_compute_raster() - Returns data frame results per x/y intersection db_compute_raster2() - Returns db_compute_raster() function plus coordinates x/y boxes db_compute_boxplot() - Returns data frame boxplot calculations data can piped plot
code: db_flights |>   db_compute_bins(arr_delay)  #> # A tibble: 28 × 2 #>    arr_delay  count #>        <dbl>  <int> #>  1     NA      9430 #>  2    -86      5325 #>  3    -40.7  207999 #>  4      4.53  79784 #>  5     49.8   19063 #>  6     95.1    7890 #>  7    140.     3746 #>  8    186.     1742 #>  9    231.      921 #> 10    276.      425 #> # ℹ 18 more rows db_flights |>   filter(arr_delay < 100 , arr_delay > -50) |>   db_compute_bins(arr_delay) |>   ggplot() +   geom_col(aes(arr_delay, count, fill = count))

title: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
text: Leverages 'dplyr' process calculations plot inside database. package provides helper functions abstract work three levels: outputs 'ggplot', outputs calculations, outputs formula needed calculate bins. dbplot package provides functions create plots using data resides databases remote data sources. leverages dplyr dbplyr push computations database, allowing visualize large datasets without loading entirely R memory.

title: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
text: ## Main Features dbplot provides three levels functionality: 1. **Plot Functions** - Functions output ggplot2 objects:    - [dbplot_histogram()] - Histogram plots    - [dbplot_bar()] - Bar plots discrete variables    - [dbplot_line()] - Line plots discrete variables    - [dbplot_raster()] - Raster/heatmap plots two continuous variables    - [dbplot_boxplot()] - Boxplot grouped continuous data 2. **Computation Functions** - Functions return data frames aggregated data:    - [db_compute_bins()] - Histogram bins counts    - [db_compute_count()] - Counts per discrete value    - [db_compute_raster()] - Aggregated data per x/y intersection    - [db_compute_raster2()] - Raster data box coordinates    - [db_compute_boxplot()] - Boxplot statistics 3. **Utility Functions** - Building blocks custom operations:    - [db_bin()] - Creates binning formulas use dplyr verbs ## Database Compatibility dbplot works database backend supported dplyr/dbplyr, including: - SQLite - PostgreSQL - MySQL/MariaDB - SQL Server - Oracle - Spark (via sparklyr) ## Minimum Requirements - R >= 4.1.0 (native pipe `|>` support) - dplyr >= 1.0.0 - ggplot2 >= 3.3.0 - rlang >= 1.0.0 - purrr (version) ## Usage Philosophy package follows principles: 1. **Push Computations Database**: aggregations happen database,    minimizing data transfer memory usage. 2. **Familiar dplyr Syntax**: Uses standard dplyr/tidyverse patterns, making    easy integrate existing workflows. 3. **Lazy Evaluation**: Leverages dplyr's lazy evaluation build efficient    database queries. 4. **ggplot2 Output**: Plot functions return ggplot2 objects can    customized using standard ggplot2 functions. ## Breaking Changes 0.4.0 Version 0.4.0 introduced breaking changes: - **longer exports `   load magrittr explicitly `library(magrittr)`. - **Minimum R version increased**: Now requires R >= 4.1.0 (previously >= 3.1). - **Modern dependencies**: Updated require dplyr >= 1.0.0, rlang >= 1.0.0,   modern package versions.
